source: Learning Rsync - rsync brief description: Backup brief: what a backup is and the backup methods

options: Duplicating file-system or database data so it can be restored after an error or disaster
Permanently deleting old data
Compressing files to save space
Encrypting a disk
questions.why: A backup duplicates data so effective data can be restored and normal operation resumed after a failure.

options: A one-time copy of all files/data (fastest restore, but the most space)
Only the files changed since yesterday
Only files new since the last full backup
Only part of the system
questions.why: A full backup copies everything at once: quickest recovery, largest space usage.

options: The data added/changed since the last full OR incremental backup
Everything, every time
Only changes since the last FULL backup
Nothing
questions.why: Each incremental backs up just what changed since the previous backup (full or incremental).

options: All the changes since the last FULL backup (growing each time)
Only changes since the previous differential
Everything each time
Nothing
questions.why: Differential backs up everything changed since the full backup, so it grows until the next full.

questions.q: What is the difference between a cold backup and a hot backup?
options: Cold is taken while the system is stopped/maintained (data consistent); hot while it runs (data may lag)
Cold is online, hot is offline
Both are taken offline
Both are taken online
questions.why: Cold = system shut down (exact data); hot = running system, so the backup lags the live data a little.

options: Backing up data to another geographic location, to survive fire/disaster/theft
Backing up to a second partition on the same disk
Keeping data in RAM
Backing up to the same directory
questions.why: Remote backup stores data off-site so a local disaster does not destroy it.

options: Backing up only a chosen part of the system
Backing up everything
Backing up only databases
Backing up only changed files
questions.why: Selective backup covers just a part of the system.
